verses.text: Nicodemus comes by night; 'Except a man be born again, he cannot see the kingdom of God.' New birth needful. None enters the kingdom but by a fresh beginning.
Nicodemus comes by night; 'Except a man be born again, he cannot see the kingdom of God.' New birth needful. None enters the kingdom but by a fresh beginning.
Nicodemus comes by night; 'Except a man be born again, he cannot see the kingdom of God.' New birth needful. None enters the kingdom but by a fresh beginning.
How be born when old? 'Except a man be born of water and of the Spirit'; that born of the Spirit is spirit. Spiritual birth. The new life is the Spirit's work, not the flesh's.
How be born when old? 'Except a man be born of water and of the Spirit'; that born of the Spirit is spirit. Spiritual birth. The new life is the Spirit's work, not the flesh's.
How be born when old? 'Except a man be born of water and of the Spirit'; that born of the Spirit is spirit. Spiritual birth. The new life is the Spirit's work, not the flesh's.
'Ye must be born again'; the wind blows where it will, so is everyone born of the Spirit. Free and unseen. The Spirit works as He pleases, His effects plain though His way is hidden.
'Ye must be born again'; the wind blows where it will, so is everyone born of the Spirit. Free and unseen. The Spirit works as He pleases, His effects plain though His way is hidden.
How can these be? art thou a master in Israel and knowest not? Earthly things unbelieved, how heavenly? Slow to grasp. The teacher of Israel must himself be taught.
How can these be? art thou a master in Israel and knowest not? Earthly things unbelieved, how heavenly? Slow to grasp. The teacher of Israel must himself be taught.

As Moses lifted the serpent, 'so must the Son of man be lifted up,' that believers have eternal life. The cross foreshadowed. To look to the crucified Christ is to live.

'God so loved the world, that he gave his only begotten Son, that whosoever believeth in him should not perish, but have everlasting life.' The gospel in one verse. Love gives, faith receives, life follows.

'God so loved the world, that he gave his only begotten Son, that whosoever believeth in him should not perish, but have everlasting life.' The gospel in one verse. Love gives, faith receives, life follows.
He that believes not is condemned already; men love darkness, 'because their deeds were evil.' Self-condemned. To refuse the light is to choose the dark.

John's disciples dispute; they tell him all men come to Jesus. Jealous for John. They grudge the rising of the greater One.

'I am not the Christ, but... sent before him'; the bridegroom's friend rejoices; 'He must increase, but I must decrease.' Glad to fade. The true servant seeks Christ's glory, not his own.

He that comes from above is above all; whoso receives His testimony 'hath set to his seal that God is true.' Trustworthy witness. To believe Christ is to honour God.
He that comes from above is above all; whoso receives His testimony 'hath set to his seal that God is true.' Trustworthy witness. To believe Christ is to honour God.
He that comes from above is above all; whoso receives His testimony 'hath set to his seal that God is true.' Trustworthy witness. To believe Christ is to honour God.
God gives not the Spirit by measure to Him; 'he that believeth on the Son hath everlasting life.' Life or wrath. Faith in the Son is the dividing line.
